interactions.summary: The host relates Jiang's wealth theory to his own deplatforming experience, and Jiang reaffirms that reputation, adaptability, and imagination are more durable than money.
The host asks how this works when one has a family to provide for, and Jiang answers with a blackout model of neighborly improvisation and anti-authoritarian resilience.

claims.claim: Jiang says a person with influence, reputation, and imagination will continue to find ways to live and work even after losing platforms or digital infrastructure.
Jiang says in crisis people naturally self-organize through intuition and cooperation rather than collapsing without formal authority.
Jiang says confidence in human resilience is stronger than confidence in laws, experts, or institutions.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang uses a blackout intersection as his image of spontaneous order without command.
signature_moments.source_phrase: people intuitively figure out what they should be doing
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns abstract trust in imagination into a concrete civic scene.
